programmes.show_description: Alf is charged to look after the Christmas club subscriptions at the pub. Unfortunately maths is not his strong point and he struggles to balance the books. S6 E6: Christmas Club Books
With BA Robertson and Mel Smith. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy show from Thames TV, all in the best possible taste!
Simon is visited by a petty crook who has become involved in a blackmail plot by a villain known only as the Scorpion. S3 E4: The Scorpion

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
Amelia Earhart inspired millions, then vanished on her Pacific flight. Her fate remains one of aviation's enduring mysteries. Narrated by Robert Powell.
Our exploration of the elite teams that have led the USA's battle against terror continues with a look at the US Special Forces and the founding of the legendary Green Berets and Navy SEALs.
From Germany's Big Bertha in World War I to Iraq's top-secret Project Babylon, we look at secret cannons designed to hit their targets from miles away. S2 E11: WW1 - Super Guns
Exploring medieval London, what Westminster Abbey reveals about the competition between the crown and the city, the cellars below the Ministry of Defence and artefacts owned by Henry VIII. Ep2
The RAF had bombers that could fly to Berlin and back by the end of the First World War, but British civilians found themselves under direct attack by Zeppelin airships. Ep4: Strike Hard, Strike Sure
This programme takes a look at the illustrious history of the Chindits, a group formed by Major General Orde Wingate to fight the Japanese in Burma. Ep11: The Chindits
Tim Pigott-Smith narrates the story of the battle for Guadalcanal and the capture of this strategically crucial island in World War 2 during which more than 25,000 Japanese lost their lives.
The story of Nazi heavy cruisers and battleships during WWII including the Bismarck, how she got into the Atlantic, and the steps taken to defeat her. Ep5: The Men Who Sank The Bismarck
...Where Were You? There are some news events that change the world. In today's episode we take you back to July 25th 2000 and the fatal crash of the Air France Concorde flight 4590.
Japan begins to conquer southeast Asia as the Allies attempt to block the empire's aggressive expansion, but ruthless tactics by Japan only help to further their war effort. Ep6: Road to Burma
A handful of names are carved into history as turning points which influenced the course of WWII. These words are indestructible: El Alamein, Okinawa, and Pearl Harbor. Ep8: Turning Points of History
After Hitler invaded Poland, the Western Allies and Nazi Germany were technically at war - for 7 months, neither side engaged in major military land operations. S1 E7: 'Sitzkrieg' and the Phoney War
King Edward VII is the guest of honour at Eaton Place and Mrs Bridges gets to do what every cook in Mayfair and Belgravia would wish to do: cook for His Royal Highness. S2 E5: Guest of Honour
After Terry goes missing, Arthur Daley returns with his nephew Ray in tow as his new minder - and ambitions to expand into the European market. S8 E1: The Loneliness of the Long Distance Entrepreneur
When drug dealers kidnap a woman's child to deter her from testifying against them, Dempsey must turn to the villainous Bogeyman for help. S3 E5: Bird of Prey
When a violent burglary occurs at Alex's in-laws' house, she comes face-to-face with their son Peter, the 14-year-old future father of Molly. S2 E5
Else sends a letter inviting everyone to Australia - everyone but Alf, that is. S6 E7: The Letter
With Billy Connolly, Tom O'Connor and The Pretenders. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy show from Thames TV, all in the best possible taste!
While he is in South America, the local police engage Simon in a crooked arms deal that intends to spark a revolution. S3 E5: The Revolution Racket

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
A famed US general was injured in a car crash in Germany, appeared to recover, then died suddenly. Accident, murder, or post war tension? Narrated by Robert Powell.
We explore some of the most infamous battles in America's fight against terror as the Vietnam war begins, we look at the activities and stories of Medal of Honor recipients and the end of the war.
Regular American units fought under rules of engagement; learn how their covert missions were carried out in North Vietnam, Laos and Cambodia. S2 E12: Vietnam - Special Operations
A look at how the West End became a place for the wealthy elite, the impact of the railways, a cholera epidemic and engineer Joseph Bazalgette saving London with his new sewerage system. Ep3
Between the wars the RAF operated elegant flying boats to patrol the far-flung corners of the Empire - the boats flew long missions over the oceans looking for enemy submarines. Ep5: By Air, By Sea
The Free French Forces led by de Gaulle refused to accept the armistice their country had signed with Germany and crossed the Channel to join the fight in Britain. Ep12: The Free French Forces
An examination of the ultimate purpose of the prehistoric monument on Salisbury Plain as a team of experts embark on a daunting task to rebuild Stonehenge as it was 4,000 years ago.
The story of the Blitz of Britain and how Nazi bombers guided to targets by electronic beams were defeated by Dr Reginald Jones in the Battle of the Beams. Ep6: The Man Who Saved Britain's Cities
...You? There are some news events that change the world - we take you back to the 26th of December, 2004 and the Indonesian tsunami.
The war takes root in Africa as an ambitious Mussolini expands the war into North Africa; he sends ill-prepared soldiers and forces Hitler to come to his ally's aid. Ep7: Desert War
Explore the events leading to one of WW2's most significant and notorious battles, the Battle of Stalingrad, and see how the USSR prepared for German attack as Germany planned to break their pact. E1
As Britain alone faced German forces with Nazi troops storming across Europe at great speed, the course of the war was far from certain. S1 E8: The Battle of Britain
An Irishman attempts to bribe Lady Marjorie with love letters she had once written; Thomas gets wrapped up in the web of deceit, but will money get the better of him? S2 E6: The Property of a Lady
Arthur ventures into the wine trade with a vintage from the Brothers of the Blessed Bidolph - a small monastic order just off the A2. S8 E2: A Bouquet of Barbed Wine
When a girl is followed home from a laundrette and abducted, Dempsey gets concerned when the man responsible seems to want Makepeace to find him. S3 E6: Out of Darkness
When a dead body in a canal is identified as a man reported missing by his glamorous wife, the investigation puts both Gene and Alex in danger. S2 E6
